title: Blessed Among Women --To The Signora Cairoli by Algernon Charles Swinburne — Love Poetry Lines
content:
Blessed was she that bare,
Hidden in flesh most fair,
For all men's sake the likeness of all love;
Holy that virgin's womb,
The old record saith, on whom
The glory of God alighted as a dove;
Blessed, who brought to gracious birth
The sweet-souled Saviour of a man-tormented earth.
meaning: "Blessed Among Women --To The Signora Cairoli" by Algernon Charles Swinburne is a love and deep poem consisting of 8 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Blessed was she that bare, Hidden in flesh most fair,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Algernon Charles Swinburne's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
